name: AHVI Calendar Predictive Life Engine
description: Predictive calendar brain for AHVI. Classifies events, adapts tone, generates reminders, prep tasks, outfit prompts, packing hints, day briefings, finance nudges, family coordination, and follow-up suggestions.

tone_engine.goal: Sound casual, human, adaptive, and helpful. Never robotic.

closers: That should make the day smoother.
One less thing to think about.

openers: Just so this doesn’t sneak up on you
Little prep now, easier day later

predictive_engine.goal: Infer what the user will need before an event and surface the most helpful next actions.

tasks: pay before due window if possible

lab_test: water or snack after if allowed

rules.buffer_reason: kids events usually take longer than expected

suggest: sync beauty prep with event timing

family_account_sync.goal: Let AHVI work like one connected household calendar brain across family accounts, while still respecting ownership, privacy, and role-based visibility.

visibility_modes.selected_members_only: Visible only to explicitly chosen members.
visibility_modes.summary_only: Shows timing and logistics but hides sensitive notes.

sync_behaviors: merge household events into a shared family timeline
keep personal/private events separate unless explicitly shared
surface duplicate or overlapping family events
assign responsibility tags for pickup, bills, gifts, travel, or check-ins
show who owns the event and who needs to act
sync prep status across linked members when relevant

rules: if two adults are assigned the same pickup, AHVI asks for confirmation
if two family events overlap, AHVI flags conflict and suggests who may need to adjust
if a shared bill is unpaid near due time, AHVI can notify the assigned payer first, then household admin if allowed
if school event and work event overlap for the same guardian, AHVI highlights coordination risk

kids_sync_rules.goal: Make child-related events visible and actionable for the right adults without cluttering everyone else's calendar.
behaviors: child events can appear on both parents' calendars when enabled
pickup and reporting-time events get higher reminder priority
school fees can appear under both kids and finance views
costume, sportswear, gifts, and forms can be synced as shared prep items
one adult can be primary owner while another gets backup visibility
shared dinners, travel, bills, and social events can appear on both calendars
joint reminders can be converted into role-specific prompts
AHVI can ask who is handling what when a shared event needs prep
shared rent, school fees, utilities, and EMIs can be tagged as household payments
assigned payer receives primary due reminders
household admin can receive escalation reminders if enabled
other members may see summary-only visibility
family trips can sync flights, hotel check-ins, packing, and wake-up reminders
AHVI can generate separate prompts for each traveler role
parents can receive child packing prompts while adults get document prompts

coordination_prompts.pickup: Quick check — who’s doing pickup today?
coordination_prompts.gift: This one may need a gift. Better to sort it before the rush.
coordination_prompts.school: Worth checking if anything extra is needed from school for this.
coordination_prompts.bill: This payment is coming up. Want to clear it early and forget about it?
coordination_prompts.shared_event: Want me to mark who’s handling this one?
coordination_prompts.conflict: There’s a family timing clash here — want to assign who’s covering what?

finance_prediction_layer.goal: Turn bill events into proactive life management instead of one-off reminders.
behaviors: cluster_same_week_payments
highlight_due_today_items
distinguish_autopay_vs_manual
surface_school_fee_under_kids_plus_finance
suggest_clearing_small_bills_together

reminders.message: Quick heads-up — your flight is tomorrow. Check in and save the boarding pass.
Pack the basics tonight so morning-you has it easy: ID, charger, meds, wallet.
Set your wake-up alarm now so this doesn’t turn chaotic in the morning.
Flight day. Keep your ID and phone somewhere obvious before you leave.
Time to head out soon. Better to be early than airport-stressed.
Final check: ID, phone, wallet, charger, luggage.
Just a heads-up — double-check passport, visa, and travel docs now instead of later.
Check in for your flight and keep the boarding pass handy.
Tonight’s packing check: passport, chargers, meds, cards, and your travel basics.
Set your alarm now. International travel has enough going on already.
Time to leave with enough buffer for immigration and check-in.
Final check: passport, visa, phone, wallet, charger, luggage.
Quick reminder — skim the agenda and open anything you’ll need for the meeting.
Meeting soon. Link, notes, and charger check.
Presentation tomorrow. Do one clean deck review tonight and call it done.
Open the final deck now, not five minutes before.
Laptop, charger, deck, notes. You’re set.
Interview tomorrow. Keep your outfit, CV, and basics ready tonight.
Quick check before interview mode: CV, link or venue, charger, and a few calm breaths.
You’re almost on. Notes open, CV ready, link checked.
Conference tomorrow. Keep your badge, charger, and anything you’ll need ready tonight.
Time to start heading out with some buffer.
This deadline is coming up. Worth getting the final bits sorted now.
Due tomorrow. A final pass today will make tomorrow lighter.
Due soon. Final check and submit when ready.
PTM tomorrow. Note down anything you want to ask so it’s all in one place.
PTM coming up. Time to head out with a little buffer.

Quick check before you leave: costume, shoes, bottle, reporting time.
Sports Day tomorrow. Keep sportswear, shoes, bottle, and any extras ready tonight.
Sports Day check: shoes, bottle, cap if needed, and leave on time.
Playdate later. Quick check for socks, water, and anything your kid needs.
Birthday party tomorrow. Keep the gift ready tonight so it’s not a last-minute scramble.
Party check: gift, outfit, socks if needed, water bottle, and go.
Pickup time soon. Time to head out.
Class later today. Keep the uniform or class items ready when you get a second.
Class starts soon. Grab the basics and head out.
Competition tomorrow. Keep everything packed tonight so the morning stays calm.
Competition check: materials, water, outfit, reporting time.
Birthday plans later. Worth deciding outfit and gift now so it’s easy later.
Party soon. Gift, phone, wallet, keys — then go have fun.
Dinner later. A polished easy look should work well here.
Dinner soon. Time to head out so you’re not rushing.
Brunch later. Something easy, fresh, and daytime-friendly should work nicely.
Brunch soon. Start getting ready.
Cocktail plans later. Good time to lock outfit, shoes, and bag.
Cocktail soon. Final check and head out.
Wedding coming up. This is the moment to finalize outfit, shoes, bag, and jewellery.
Steam or iron the outfit tonight and keep everything together.
Wedding later. Give yourself enough getting-ready time so nothing feels rushed.
Wedding soon. Outfit, shoes, bag, gift, phone, wallet — then out you go.
Reception tomorrow. Good time to set your outfit and accessories aside.
Reception soon. Final check and head out.
Date later. Pick something that feels like you and doesn’t need fussing with.
Time to get going soon.
Family plans later. Keep the easy basics ready and head out on time.
Doctor appointment tomorrow. Keep reports, ID, and any notes in one place tonight.
Appointment soon. Reports, wallet, phone, and time to head out.
Dentist appointment soon. Time to head out.
Lab test tomorrow. If fasting is needed, keep that in mind tonight.
Test soon. Reports if any, ID, water after if needed, and go.

autopay_messages.-1d: This gets auto-paid tomorrow. Just make sure the account is ready.
autopay_messages.0d: This should auto-pay today. Worth a quick glance later to be sure it went through.

example_1.title: Aarav Annual Day
ahvi_behavior: show event on both parents' calendars
send costume prep reminder to mom

example_2.title: Rent Payment
ahvi_behavior: send primary due reminders to assigned payer
show summary in shared finance view
optionally escalate to admin on due day if unpaid

example_3.title: Family Goa Trip
ahvi_behavior: sync same trip across family accounts
separate document prompts from child packing prompts
show one household trip briefing with role-based prep

notes: AHVI Calendar v3 rule set fully defined. Predictive engine, tone engine, classification, rule packs, and developer pipeline are complete.
